title: INSERT — add a new order
instruction:
The company database is live — you can add new rows and immediately query them.

Insert a new order for Acme Corp and verify it appears in their order history.
starter:
-- Add a new order
INSERT INTO orders (id, customer_id, product, amount, order_date, status)
VALUES (19, 1, 'Enterprise Plan', 24000.00, '2025-01-15', 'pending');

-- Verify Acme Corp's complete order history
SELECT o.product, o.amount, o.order_date, o.status
FROM orders o
JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id
WHERE c.name = 'Acme Corp'
ORDER BY o.order_date;
successMessage: The new order (id=19) appears immediately. Acme Corp now has 4 orders — 2 paid, 2 pending. Notice the 2025 renewal is pending; it hasn't been paid yet. INSERT and SELECT work together — no delay.

title: UPDATE — mark an order as paid
instruction:
`UPDATE` modifies existing rows. **Always include a WHERE clause** — without it you'd update every row in the table.

Mark Acme Corp's pending training package as paid.
starter:
-- Mark order 16 (Acme Corp Training Package) as paid
UPDATE orders
SET status = 'paid',
    order_date = '2025-01-20'
WHERE id = 16;

-- Verify the change
SELECT id, product, amount, order_date, status
FROM orders
WHERE id = 16;
successMessage: Order 16 is now paid with an updated date. The `WHERE id = 16` is critical — without it, ALL 18 orders would have been updated to `paid`. Always confirm with a SELECT before running UPDATE or DELETE.

title: DELETE — remove stale pending orders
instruction:
`DELETE` removes rows permanently. Best practice: run a SELECT first to preview exactly which rows you'll delete, then replace SELECT with DELETE.

Find and remove orders that have been pending for over a year (before 2024-01-01).
starter:
-- Step 1: preview what would be deleted
SELECT id, customer_id, product, amount, order_date
FROM orders
WHERE status = 'pending'
  AND order_date < '2024-01-01';

-- (In a real scenario, run the DELETE after confirming)
-- DELETE FROM orders WHERE status = 'pending' AND order_date < '2024-01-01';
successMessage: Only order 11 (Vertex Solutions' Pro Plan) matches — it's been pending since February 2024. The preview pattern (SELECT first) is a safety habit every data engineer follows before running DELETE. Uncomment the DELETE to actually remove it.
